patches.find: Under the dome author's own definition ("prediction was timestamped before confirming data was pulled"), the hardcoded expected value sits in the same commit as the live fetch call, making even that weaker test unverifiable.
patches.replace: Under the dome author's own criterion (“Timestamped before confirming data”), that framing doesn’t even apply here: the dome lists no live fetch for this domain at all (monitoring: hardcoded) — the 104–140 deg “observation” is a static string in monitor.py, so there is no data-pull event to timestamp.
patches.rationale: H1 (major): removes contradiction between monitoring:hardcoded tag and prose reference to 'live fetch call'. H2 (moderate): fixes paraphrased dome quote by restoring verbatim 'Timestamped before confirming data' text. Combined single-sentence replacement.

patches.find: “Chebychev low-pass [attenuation of] contact-phase transients before Level-3 release.”
patches.replace: “Chebychev low-pass attenuates contact-phase transients before Level-3 release”
patches.rationale: H4 (moderate): removes bracketed verb modification '[attenuation of]' that transforms dome's verbatim verb 'attenuates' into a gerund. Uses verbatim dome text. Also removes internal period before closing quote (not in original).

patches.find: revising the earlier Wang et al. 2000 preliminary report
patches.replace: revising the earlier Wang et al. 2000 report (Phys. Rev. D 62, 041101(R))
patches.rationale: H5 (moderate): Wang et al. (2000) was a peer-reviewed Rapid Communication in Phys. Rev. D, not a preliminary report. 'Preliminary' is rhetorically tendentious and invites citation criticism. Adding the actual journal reference improves factual accuracy.

patches.find: retreating to a “thermal-tilt loophole” for non-superconducting instruments as the only surviving mechanism
patches.replace: acknowledging that Unnikrishnan et al. (2001) rule out gravitational shielding and that Yang (2002)’s tilt experiment “did not fully rule out” thermal tilt as a mechanism for the Mohe −6.5 µGal signal
patches.rationale: H6 (moderate): 'thermal-tilt loophole as the only surviving mechanism' overclaims — the dome's live WIN-011 now presents a dielectric boundary transition model as its primary mechanism, not thermal tilt. The replacement correctly characterizes the dome's actual current position.

patches.find: built to suppress. The dome claims κ = 1.67 appears in both eclipse data (WIN-012) and geomagnetic storm data (Exhibit A), but provides no mechanism explaining why the same coupling ratio should operate in fundamentally different physical scenarios — and its own eclipse prediction model (E-PRED-C) predicts 0.0 µGal during eclipses.
patches.replace: built to suppress. The dome’s live WIN-013 now adds the escape clause “measurements were not optimized for C1/C4 timing correlation” and WIN-014 adds “the 2009 campaign searched for totality-peak or shielding-style effects, not the contact-phase kappa-scaled pulse the ECM now predicts.” Both retcon the SG nulls as non-tests of the current prediction. Both are scientifically weak: SG data from 1999 (Membach) and 2009 (Chinese network) is continuously sampled across the full eclipse including C1/C4, and a −10 to −13 µGal pulse over a few minutes at contact would exceed the SG noise floor. The retcon is indistinguishable from an unfalsifiable retreat: any prior null becomes “the wrong test” when the prediction moves. The dome claims κ = 1.67 appears in both eclipse data (WIN-012) and geomagnetic storm data (Exhibit A), but provides no mechanism explaining why the same coupling ratio should operate in fundamentally different physical scenarios — and the dome’s own current eclipse gravity prediction (PRED-TIER-2, predictions.html, accessed 2026-04-21) calls for a −10 to −13 µGal downward pulse at C1/C4 contact phases, a value incompatible with BOTH the 1997 Mohe −6.5 µGal datum (50–100% discrepancy, same instrument class) AND the 1999/2009 SG nulls. Furthermore, κ cannot simultaneously yield the dome’s historical −6.5 µGal “confirmation” (WIN-012) and the dome’s −10 to −13 µGal forward prediction (PRED-TIER-2): at κ = 1.67 nT/µGal, ΔB/κ yields −10.2 to −12.6 µGal — consistent with PRED-TIER-2 but incompatible with the Mohe datum that anchors κ itself. This is a novel self-contradiction: the coupling constant’s denominator disagrees with the forward prediction that uses it.
patches.rationale: H1 (critical): removes fabricated 'E-PRED-C' label (verified not present on dome site 2026-04-21), replaces with verified PRED-TIER-2 reference with quantitative discrepancy. H3 (major): adds dome's new escape clauses for WIN-013/014 and rebuts them. H7 (moderate): adds kappa self-contradiction computation (kappa yields PRED-TIER-2 range but this is 50-100% larger than Mohe datum that defines kappa).

patches.find: the ionospheric parameters Sq models use (E-region electron density, conductivity tensors) are measured independently by ionosondes and satellite instruments — not derived from the eclipse signal being predicted.
patches.replace: the ionospheric parameters Sq models use (E-region electron density, conductivity tensors) are obtained from independent sources — ionosonde networks, GPS/TEC, and climatological averages — none of which are fitted to the eclipse magnetic signal being predicted.
patches.rationale: H1 (moderate): tightens 'measured independently by ionosondes and satellite instruments' to be accurate: modern Sq models use climatological averages, not eclipse-time measurements. The replacement avoids overstating independence while preserving the circularity contrast.

patches.find: Counting the same observation three times inflates the WIN count.
patches.replace: The dome counts each as a separate confirmed prediction, inflating the WIN total to three entries for what remains a single non-discriminating mechanism — three observational confirmations of Chapman Sq, not three independent dome-derived predictions.
patches.rationale: H2 (moderate): 'inflating the score count without adding independent evidence' is inaccurate — the three events ARE independent observations. The replacement concedes legitimate observational independence while sharpening the 'non-discriminating' critique (which is the stronger attack).
